questions.question: Which of the following is a key advantage of projective tests over self-report inventories?

options.text: They have higher reliability and validity
They are less susceptible to deliberate impression management
They are easier to administer and score
They produce more objective, standardized results

questions.explanation: Because the 'correct' response to a projective test is ambiguous, individuals cannot easily manipulate their responses to create a favorable impression, unlike self-report inventories where socially desirable responses may be obvious.

questions.question: The MMPI is best described as:

options.text: A projective test that uses inkblots to assess unconscious processes
A behavioral observation checklist used by clinicians
A self-report inventory designed to assess psychological disorders and personality characteristics
A measure of the Big Five personality traits exclusively

questions.explanation: The Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory (MMPI) is a widely used self-report inventory containing hundreds of true/false items that produce scores on clinical scales and validity scales, making it a key tool in clinical assessment.
